name: Älvsbyn (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the municipal arms
gold (Or) — the sun, the triple-mount, and the wavy base

description: A blue field bearing a golden sun above a golden triple-mount that spans the width, issuant from a wavy base — the banner of the arms of Älvsbyn Municipality.
history: The municipal arms were composed by the Swedish State Heraldry Office (Riksheraldikerämbetet) and established for Älvsby köping on 17 June 1948, then re-registered for Älvsbyn Municipality (formed 1971) in 1974. Blazon: azure, a throughout triple-mount issuant from a wavy base and, above it, a sun, all Or. The sun is an old alchemical symbol for gold, alluding to a legendary gold deposit noted in the region on Olaus Magnus's 16th-century Carta Marina; the triple-mount and wavy base stand for the district's mountains and its river (Piteälven). Älvsbyn has no separately adopted municipal flag; the flag shown is the banner of these arms.

features.symbolism: the azure field of the municipal arms

features.role: a throughout wavy-cut triple-mount (treberg) issuant from a wavy base, spanning the width of the flag
features.symbolism: the mountains of the district and, in the wavy base, its river (Piteälven)

features.role: a sun without a face, placed in chief above the triple-mount
features.symbolism: an old alchemical symbol for the element gold, alluding to a legendary gold deposit recorded in the district
